message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit() per PR: edit_file->edit_files, filename accepts str or iterable

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority parameter to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

type: INTENT
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to Editor.__init__/edit_file and click.edit(); will keep edit_file() call signature stable at call site, just add priority kwarg - please keep edit_file/edit_files rename compatible with an added process_priority param so we don't clobber each other

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: renamed edit_file(filename:str) to edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in Editor class, called as self.edit_files((name,)) from edit(). click.edit() filename param now accepts str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, wrapped into tuple before calling ed.edit_files(filenames). Feel free to add process_priority as an additional kwarg to Editor.__init__ and edit_files signature - just keep filenames as first positional arg.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: added process_priority param to Editor.__init__/edit_file (sets OS priority via os.setpriority/SetPriorityClass, best-effort) and click.edit(). Did not touch edit_file->edit_files rename, go ahead and layer your multi-file changes on top.
